region_of_origin: United States (Coca-Cola brand; widely consumed in Singapore)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'iced tea' class (FOODON_03301103); no specific Fuze Tea branded entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0KL covers tea-based beverages as a category; no branded Fuze Tea entry exists in FoodEx2. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 34111008 represents tea as a substance; no specific iced lemon tea or Fuze Tea branded concept exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea is approximately 500 ml (500 g), equivalent to one retail bottle or a large cup as typically served at a food outlet.

nutrient_source.title: Fuze Tea Lemon Tea — Nutrition Facts (Coca-Cola USDA Branded Food submission)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Fuze Tea Lemon Tea branded product data sourced from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods database (Coca-Cola Company submission). Values are per 100 ml (approximately equivalent to per 100 g for this aqueous beverage, density ~1.0 g/ml). Typical Fuze Tea Lemon Tea per 100 ml: energy ~19 kcal, carbohydrate ~4.7 g (of which sugars ~4.5 g), protein 0 g, fat 0 g, sodium ~5 mg, dietary fibre 0 g. Values adapted from USDA Branded Food entry for Fuze Tea Lemon Tea.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sweetened iced tea and lemon tea beverages — University of Sydney GI Database reference for tea-based sweetened beverages
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 28 based on the closest analogous entry: sweetened iced tea / lemon tea beverages with similar sugar content (~4–5 g per 100 ml). The University of Sydney GI Database lists sweetened tea beverages in the low GI range (GI approximately 25–35). A GI of 28 is used as a conservative central estimate. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving = (28 × 23.5 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 6.6; however, per the full 500 g serving size: GL = (28 × 23.5) / 100 ≈ 6.6. Note: GL per serving recalculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (28 × 23.5) / 100 = 6.6. Value stored as 6.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~25 mg per 500 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea contains approximately 25 mg sodium per 500 ml serving, which is well within safe limits for hypertensive individuals; however, regular consumption of sweetened beverages should be moderated as part of an overall dietary pattern to support blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Free sugar content (~22.5 g per 500 ml serving) contributing to postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Each 500 ml serving of Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea provides approximately 22.5 g of free sugars, which can raise postprandial blood glucose; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption and consider sugar-free or unsweetened tea alternatives to better manage glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and sodium; moderate free sugar intake
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea contains no fat or cholesterol and very low sodium, posing minimal direct cardiovascular risk from lipid or sodium load; however, the free sugar content (~22.5 g per 500 ml) warrants moderation as excessive sugar intake is associated with elevated triglycerides and increased cardiovascular risk over time.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea is a ready-to-drink (RTD) sweetened iced tea beverage produced by the Coca-Cola Company under the Fuze Tea brand. It is made from water, tea extract, lemon juice or lemon flavouring, sugar, citric acid, and natural flavourings. The beverage is served chilled and has a light, refreshing taste with a mild tea flavour balanced by a sweet-tart lemon note. The texture is thin and watery, typical of RTD tea drinks. In Singapore, iced lemon tea is a widely popular beverage available at food courts, cafes, and restaurants, and Fuze Tea is one of the commercially recognised branded versions. Nutritionally, a 500 ml serving provides approximately 95 kcal, 23.5 g of carbohydrates (predominantly free sugars at ~22.5 g), negligible protein and fat, and very low sodium (~25 mg). The beverage has a low glycaemic index (~28) but a moderate glycaemic load per serving due to the volume of sugar consumed. It is suitable for vegetarians and vegans and is generally considered halal. Clinically, the primary concern is the free sugar content, which is relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, obesity, or metabolic syndrome. It is not a significant source of micronutrients. Consumers seeking a lower-sugar option should consider unsweetened or zero-sugar iced tea alternatives.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea?
Is Fuze Tea Ice Lemon Tea healthy?
How much sugar is in Fuze Tea lemon tea?
Is Fuze Tea suitable for diabetics?
What is the GI of iced lemon tea?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods data for Fuze Tea Lemon Tea, which is the closest approved-source match for this commercially branded product. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened tea beverages, as no direct Fuze Tea GI measurement is published. Confidence is medium rather than high because the exact Singapore-market formulation may differ slightly from the USDA-submitted product, and GI is estimated rather than directly measured.
notes: 1. Protein_g is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods data for Fuze Tea Lemon Tea; this is a tea-based aqueous beverage containing no protein by formulation. Fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation — contains no fat. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all confirmed zero by product formulation. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as this is a filtered liquid beverage with no fibre. 2. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA Branded Food source entry for this product. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 3. GI set to 28 as an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened tea beverages; gi_category is low. Glycemic load per 500 ml serving = (28 × 23.5) / 100 = 6.6. 4. The food_name has been normalised to remove the trademark symbol (™) for canonical database use. 5. This product is served at Khao - Funan (field survey data) at SGD 4.00. 6. Multilingual names follow Singaporean beverage conventions: Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are all provided as this product is widely consumed in Singapore across all language communities.
